framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Codify the firm's thinking into a teachable method
paths.what_it_is: Distill the internal problem-structuring or communication technique you use on cases into a written method precise enough that someone else can pick it up and apply it without you in the room.
paths.why_it_signals: The exact same move recurs across the profession's most-cited texts: Barbara Minto turned McKinsey's internal writing discipline into the Pyramid Principle, Ethan Rasiel turned McKinsey's case technique into The McKinsey Way, and Marc Cosentino turned the case-cracking process into Case in Point — each now a standard reference used across the industry, not just at one firm.

paths.path: Turn client-work patterns into a public strategy book
paths.what_it_is: Take the recurring pattern you keep seeing across client engagements and generalize it into a named framework and a book written for executives outside the firm, not just for fellow consultants.
paths.why_it_signals: Three practicing strategy partners independently made this move and each framework became business-canon in its own right: Roger Martin's five-choices cascade from Playing to Win, Chris Zook's core-business framework from Profit from the Core, and Adrian Slywotzky's value-migration model from The Profit Zone — all written while the author was still an active partner at a major strategy firm.

paths.path: Build a public platform teaching the recruiting craft
paths.what_it_is: After learning the case-interview and problem-solving craft as a practicing consultant, build an openly public teaching platform (site, book, workshop series) that trains outsiders in that same craft at scale.
paths.why_it_signals: Victor Cheng (ex-McKinsey), David Ohrvall (ex-Bain), and Jenny Rae Le Roux (ex-Bain) each left practicing consulting to build a named training platform — CaseInterview.com, Zintervu, and Management Consulted respectively — that now trains tens of thousands of candidates a year, showing the underlying craft is genuinely teachable, not just personal talent.
